title: Hawke's Bay in May
meta_desc: Plan your family holiday in Hawke's Bay this May. Enjoy mild weather, Art Deco Napier, Cape Kidnappers gannets, and NZ's best fruit. Budget $180-340/day for a.
intro: May is a great time to visit Hawke's Bay with kids, with mild autumn weather and a range of exciting activities to enjoy. The region is perfect for families with children aged 5+, with a mix of outdoor adventures, cultural experiences, and relaxing beach time. From Cape Kidnappers to Napier's Art Deco city, there's something for everyone in Hawke's Bay.

sections.heading: Weather in May
sections.body:
May is a lovely time to visit Hawke's Bay, with autumn in full swing. The weather is generally mild, with average highs of 18°C and lows of 9°C. It's a great time to enjoy the outdoors, with comfortable temperatures for walking, biking, or swimming. Be sure to pack layers for cooler mornings and evenings.

With an average of 9 hours of sunshine per day, May is an ideal time to explore Hawke's Bay's beautiful beaches, such as Ocean Beach and Waimarama Beach. The sea temperature is still warm enough for swimming, making it perfect for families with kids.

sections.heading: What's On in May
sections.body:
May is a great time to experience Hawke's Bay's events and festivals. The region hosts a range of activities, from food and wine festivals to outdoor concerts and markets. Be sure to check the local calendar to catch some of the exciting events on offer.

The Napier Art Deco weekend is a highlight of the month, with a range of activities and events celebrating the city's unique Art Deco heritage. Take a guided tour, attend a lecture, or simply enjoy the city's beautiful architecture.
list: Hawke's Bay Food and Wine Festival

sections.heading: Best Activities in May
sections.body:
May is a great time to enjoy Hawke's Bay's top activities, from visiting the Cape Kidnappers Gannet Colony to exploring the Napier Aquarium. The region's beautiful beaches are perfect for swimming, surfing, or simply relaxing. Don't miss the opportunity to pick strawberries at a local farm, a fun and delicious activity for kids.

The Cape Kidnapper tractor trek is a must-do experience, offering a unique and dramatic way to access the gannet colony. Book ahead with Gannet Beach Adventures to secure your spot.
list: Cape Kidnappers Gannet Colony: $40-$60 per person

sections.heading: Practical Tips for Families
sections.body:
When visiting Hawke's Bay with kids, it's essential to plan ahead and book activities in advance. The region can get busy, especially during school holidays, so be sure to secure your spot at popular attractions.

Consider staying in Napier or nearby Hastings, which offer a range of accommodation options to suit all budgets. Don't forget to pack layers for cooler mornings and evenings, as well as sunscreen and hats for outdoor adventures.
list: Bring a picnic lunch to enjoy at the beach
Allow 30-40 minutes to drive to Waimarama Beach from Napier
